statements.statement: Every word depends on the words before it.
statements.imageAlt: Diagram showing how AI reads your context and predicts the most likely next word from many possibilities
statements.explanation:
AI does not understand your question and then write a reply. It predicts one word at a time. It looks at all the words that came before, and picks the most likely next word. Then it looks at everything again, including the word it just picked, and predicts the next one. This keeps going until the full response is built.

This means every word AI has seen so far shapes what comes next. The previous words are the context. Change the context, change the output.

This is why what you type matters so much. Your words are the context AI uses to predict everything that follows.

experiment.intro: Open ChatGPT and try these three prompts in separate conversations:

prompts.label: Prompt A
prompts.text: Write a paragraph about a bank.

prompts.label: Prompt B
prompts.text: I am a fisherman. Write a paragraph about a bank.

prompts.label: Prompt C
prompts.text: I am a financial advisor. Write a paragraph about a bank.

experiment.result:
Notice how the same word "bank" produces completely different results. In prompt A, AI could go either way. In prompt B, it writes about a river bank. In prompt C, it writes about a financial bank.

You did not change the question. You changed the context. And that changed everything.

children.statement: It does not know when it is wrong.
children.explanation: Because AI is just predicting the next word, it has no idea if what it wrote is true or false. A correct fact and a made up fact look exactly the same to it. It will cite a book that does not exist with the same confidence as a real one. Always verify anything that matters.

terms.hallucination: When AI confidently writes something that is completely made up. It is not trying to lie. It just cannot tell the difference between real and fake.

children.statement: It learned from everything we wrote, good and bad.
children.explanation: AI was trained on billions of pages written by people. That includes great writing, but also stereotypes, outdated views, and plain wrong information. It does not filter any of this out. It just picks whatever pattern fits best.

terms.bias: When AI consistently leans one way because the text it learned from leaned that way. It reflects human writing, the good parts and the bad parts.
